mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust corporate earnings potential, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved cash flow expectations.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5% YoY but 20-day SPX returns are negative in 60%+ of cases

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate (UNRATE) decline exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals tightening labor markets, prompting inflationary pressures that lead to negative S&P 500 returns through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines but 20-day SPX returns remain positive in 70%+ of instances

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and falling federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicate accommodative policy supporting economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through multiple expansion.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth > 2% and FEDFUNDS decline < 0.5% but 60-day SPX returns < 0

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) above 3.5% signal monetary tightening, creating headwinds for equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days through increased borrowing costs.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS > 3.5% but 5-day SPX returns exceed 1.5% in 50%+ of cases

mechanism_narrative: Increasing real sales (RSAFS) reflect strong consumer demand, driving corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved earnings outlooks.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth exceeds 2% but 20-day SPX returns are negative in 60%+ of cases

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1.3 million units signal strong demand for construction, boosting related sectors and producing positive S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days through sector rotation effects.
failure_condition: HOUST > 1300.0 but 20-day SPX returns < 0 in 50%+ of instances

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicate a tightening labor market, creating inflationary pressures that lead to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through rate hike expectations.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth > 150k and UNRATE decline > 0.2pp but 5-day SPX returns > 1%

mechanism_narrative: Declining GDP (GDPC1) during a recessionary phase indicates deteriorating economic conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through earnings downgrades and risk-off sentiment.
failure_condition: GDPC1 decline > 1% but 60-day SPX returns exceed -5% in 40%+ of cases
